description: generate a CT image from an existing MAISI label mask (GPU image-from-mask inference)
rules: thin wrapper: invokes upstream python -m scripts.infer_image_from_mask — do not reimplement diffusion/decoding

REQUEST_JSON must contain mask_path pointing at an integer-like MAISI NIfTI label map; allowed extra keys are num_inference_steps, autoencoder_sliding_window_infer_size/overlap, cfg_guidance_scale, modality

the wrapper validates the mask before inference: integer-like voxels, all labels within the MAISI vocabulary (0..132 plus 200), at least one foreground label, and the label-200 body envelope (relax with ALLOW_MISSING_BODY_LABEL=1)
PREFLIGHT=1 (default) validates the mask + weights/CUDA presence and exits without inference; set PREFLIGHT=0 (and CONFIRM=1 for high step counts) to actually generate the image
outputs result.json plus *_image.nii.gz under record_store; configs are staged under the output dir so the upstream clone is never mutated
needs the CT image/controlnet weights, CUDA, and an NVIDIA GPU — there is no CPU fallback
usage: Set REQUEST_JSON (path to a JSON with mask_path + optional overrides) and NV_GENERATE_ROOT. Default run is a mask-validation preflight report; pass PREFLIGHT=0 to run inference. Output: result.json with per-image HU/geometry summaries.
limitation: Thin wrapper — do not modify code under NV_GENERATE_ROOT. Image-from-mask inference is GPU-heavy. Output is synthetic and NOT clinically meaningful. Not for clinical deployment, interpretation, autonomous diagnosis, or regulatory submission.
